claim_killed: No standard invariant distinguishes an oriented hyperbolic 3-manifold from its mirror at any genus -- the chirality firewall is genus-general; the genus-2 hope that some higher invariant breaks it is refuted by the theorem.

evidence_quote: The load-bearing content is the **standard orientation-reversal theorem** (genus-independent): for an oriented hyperbolic 3-manifold `M`, the mirror `M̄` has **same volume, opposite Chern–Simons, conjugate-isomorphic trace field**
citation_chain: B139: genus-1 mechanism (M->M^T relabeling) is present there, yes -- but genus-1 only; the genus-general closure rests on the external orientation-reversal theorem (Neumann-Zagier), not an arc computation -> authority-external.
revival_hypothesis: the EMITTANCE/spectral face B735 -- the arc's own residual caveat is exactly 'not a proof that no cleverer invariant distinguishes'; no pre-B735 negative consulted the spectral/emittance face, a candidate orientation-sensitive distinguisher.
